channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors affecting ChatGPT
item.description: Status: MonitoringService has been restored and we are continuing to monitor.Affected components Voice mode (Operational) Conversations (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) ChatGPT Work (Operational)

item.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description: Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware. According to the Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA), the activity has been attributed to...

item.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to the exploitation of recently disclosed SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series VPN appliances as zero-days prior their public disclosure since June 22, 2026. Cybersecurity company Volexity is tracking the activity under the...

item.title: AI Phones Are Not Competing on Models: The Real Battle Is Over the User Entry Point, and Super Apps Hold the Key
item.description: WAIC 2026 reveals AI phone industry consensus: the real competition is not model capability but agentic OS entry points, system-level permissions, and who controls service distribution away from super apps.

item.title: China MAZU AI Weather System to Reach 30 Countries: Fengyun Satellites, AI Models, and Radar Unite as Meteorological Infrastructure Goes Global
item.description: President Xi announces MAZU intelligent early warning system deployment to 30 countries at WAIC 2026 as China exports integrated meteorological infrastructure combining Fengyun satellites, AI, and radar.

item.title: To Make Chips, First Build a Vacuum World: How Extreme Vacuum Technology Enables Modern Semiconductor Manufacturing
item.description: From Magdeburg hemispheres to EUV lithography, semiconductor manufacturing requires extreme vacuum environments to prevent contamination, enable precision etching, and stabilize plasma for sub-nanometer chip production.

item.title: Huawei Cloud Unveils Enterprise AI at WAIC: Zhou Yuefeng Breaks Down the Steps for Scaling AI Agents Into Production Systems
item.description: Huawei Cloud CEO Zhou Yuefeng unveils AgentArts platform and openJiuwen framework at WAIC 2026, targeting enterprise-grade agent deployment with stability, security, and multi-agent coordination.

item.title: Hong Kong Cyberport Is Becoming the Springboard for Chinese AI Going Global: From Computing Power to Standards to Markets
item.description: Hong Kong Cyberport emerges as China AI globalization hub: 3000 PFLOPS AI supercomputing center, 30B HKD funding program, and three-board strategy connecting mainland AI to Southeast Asia and Middle East.

item.title: Workers and Pages builds stuck intializing.
item.description: Jul 18, 21:00 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare is aware of an issue initializing Workers & Pages Builds from 22:05 - 22:45 UTC. The problem has now been resolved.

item.title: Workers and Pages builds unable to start
item.description: Jul 18, 16:46 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue impacting Workers & Pages Builds from starting. We are working to analyze and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Some users are unable to access Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components CLI (Degraded performance) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Degraded performance)

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day

item.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description: An anonymous HTTP request can run code on a WordPress site. The bug is in core, so a bare install with zero plugins is exploitable. Every 6.9 and 7.0 site was in range until Friday, when WordPress shipped 6.9.5 and 7.0.2 and enabled what it calls forced updates through its auto-update system. Adam...

item.title: Codex 5.6-sol Experiencing Increased Server-Overload Errors
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process restarts. OpenSSL shipped the HollowByte fix in June with no CVE, no advisory, and no changelog entry...

item.title: Cache Reserve users may see increased write errors in the APAC region
item.description: Jul 17, 19:50 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 18:11 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 18:06 UTC Investigating - We are investigating an increase in write errors for the R2 cache reserve service

item.title: Elevated errors across Fable 5
item.description: Jul 17, 19:43 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved, and Claude Fable 5 is available once again without usage credits, as expected. Jul 17, 18:48 UTC Monitoring - We have applied a fix to resolve the erroneous requirement for usage credits on Fable 5, and are monitoring to ensure no further...

item.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack. The malicious package campaign, codenamed ViteVenom by Checkmarx, marks an expansion of ChainVeil, which was observed using an...

item.title: Elevated error rates for Sonnet 5
item.description: Jul 17, 18:27 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 18:22 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 18:13 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jul 17, 18:12 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this...

item.title: POST requests not succeeding
item.description: Jul 17, 16:45 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 16:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 15:50 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 17, 15:06 UTC Update - We are continuing to...

item.title: Containers Logs Issues
item.description: Jul 17, 16:40 UTC Investigating - A subset of Containers logs for Containers applications that are configured with 'observability.enabled: true' are getting dropped.

item.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 17, 15:33 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 15:27 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 14:57 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: New ChatGPT App Not Available For Enterprise Users Without Codex Permissions
item.description: Status: IdentifiedUsers who do not have Codex RBAC enabled, are getting the "codex_cli_workspace_disabled" error when trying to log into the new ChatGPT app post-install. We are working on implementing a fix. Please use our ChatGPT Classic as we are working on a fix.

item.title: In Other News: Iran Tracks US Military Phones, CrashStealer macOS Malware, CVD Blueprint
item.description: Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: OpenClaw AI agents exploited via WhatsApp, ransomware hits naval defense firm TKMS, Lidl discloses data breach. The post In Other News: Iran Tracks US Military Phones, CrashStealer macOS Malware, CVD Blueprint appeared first on...

item.title: Podcast: Broken Governance, Agentic AI, and the MindStone Agent Exclusive
item.description: (Video) Artificial intelligence is transforming cybersecurity, but are governance, compliance, and security practices evolving fast enough to keep up? The post Podcast: Broken Governance, Agentic AI, and the MindStone Agent Exclusive appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: perimenopause misinformation and China’s latest AI leap
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it. Perimenopause used to be considered taboo, but not anymore. Thanks at least in part to TV...

item.title: Beacon Security Raises $13 Million for Security Data Platform
item.description: The startup helps organizations detect, hunt, and protect their assets across environments at machine speed. The post Beacon Security Raises $13 Million for Security Data Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Industry Reactions to Pentagon Suspending CMMC Phase 2: Feedback Friday
item.description: Industry professionals broadly agree that the suspension pauses third-party CMMC audits but not the underlying legal obligation to protect CUI. The post Industry Reactions to Pentagon Suspending CMMC Phase 2: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cyberattack Disrupts Operations of Japanese Frozen Food Giant Nichirei
item.description: The company disconnected its systems on July 13 and is starting to gradually restore operations. The post Cyberattack Disrupts Operations of Japanese Frozen Food Giant Nichirei appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.
item.description: Perimenopause has entered the chat. Perimenopause—and its better-known relative, menopause—used to be considered taboo. Not anymore, thanks at least in part to TV doctors and social media influencers. Perhaps it’s my age, but these days, both my algorithm and my conversations with friends...

item.title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
item.description: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in many industries,...

item.title: Elevated errors on Sonnet 5 and Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 17, 07:10 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 17, 07:03 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 06:47 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: AI-Native Insurance for Agentic AI: Pricing, Underwriting, and End-to-End Automation
item.description: arXiv:2607.13230v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI introduces new insurance challenges because autonomous AI systems can make decisions, invoke tools, modify external environments, and interact with third-party services. This paper develops an AI-native mathematical framework for...

item.title: Cost-Optimal Foundation Model Deployment Portfolio for Transportation Management
item.description: arXiv:2607.13239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models, including large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), are increasingly used for transportation management center (TMC) tasks such as anomaly detection, incident reporting, and traveler information. Deploying...

item.title: Harness Handbook: Making Evolving Agent Harnesses Readable,Navigable, and Editable
item.description: arXiv:2607.13285v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The capability of a modern AI agent depends not only on its foundation model but also on its harness, which constructs prompts, manages state, invokes tools, and coordinates execution. As models, APIs, environments, and requirements evolve, the...

item.title: Theory-Level Autoformalization: From Isolated Statements to Unified Formal Knowledge Bases
item.description: arXiv:2607.13292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoformalization translates informal natural language into formal, machine-verifiable languages. While most work focuses on individual statements, real formalization efforts are inherently theory-level: they require an entire web of axioms,...

item.title: EZSMT Version 3, Matured
item.description: arXiv:2607.13344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Constraint Answer Set Programming (CASP) is a hybrid reasoning paradigm that combines Answer Set Programming (ASP) with Constraint Processing and Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT), enabling powerful declarative encodings of complex combinatorial...

item.title: Position: Explainability Research Must Prioritize Foundations over Ad-hoc Methods
item.description: arXiv:2607.14123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the proliferation of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques -- from feature attributions to sparse autoencoders -- explanations rarely influence real-world workflows. In practice, they are often generated and discarded without guiding meaningful...

item.title: CARPRT: Class-Aware Zero-Shot Prompt Reweighting for Black-Box Vision-Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.14125v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) enable zero-shot image classification by computing the similarity score between an image and textual descriptions, typically formed by inserting a class label (e.g., "cat") into a prompt (e.g., "a photo of...

item.title: Explainable Geospatial AI for Satellite Ground Station Siting Using LiDAR-Derived Terrain Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2607.14127v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representative clutter height (RCH) is a key parameter in radio propagation and interference analysis because it captures the dominant height of local obstructions that drive terminal clutter loss. Current practice often relies on fixed clutter...

item.title: Certified Domain Consistency for Multi-Domain Retrieval: Label-Free Per-Domain Contamination Control with Conformal Risk Guarantees
item.description: arXiv:2607.14157v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval over corpora that mix several domains often returns relevant but wrong-domain evidence that ranking metrics miss and that conformal risk control bounds only marginally, under-covering the worst domains. This work introduces C3R, a drop-in...

item.title: QFireNet: A Quantum-Enhanced U-Net for Wildfire Segmentation from Sentinel-2 Imagery
item.description: arXiv:2607.14160v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wildfire detection from satellite imagery is a semantic image segmentation problem that has proven to be difficult due to challenges such as class imbalance, feature complexity, and atmospheric interference. In this paper, we build on the foundational...

item.title: Just Keep Prompting: Evaluating Repetitive Socratic Prompting in VLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.14099v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deploying Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in real-world settings requires not only strong visual reasoning but also stability under sustained conversational pressure. We introduce Just Keep Prompting (JKP), a multi-turn evaluation framework that...

item.title: Quantum Compositional NLP for Arabic: Grammar, Morphology, and Word Sense in Circuit Topology
item.description: arXiv:2607.14100v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first application of pregroup grammar-based quantum compositional natural language processing (QNLP) to Arabic; a morphologically rich, free-word-order language whose structural complexity provides a uniquely demanding testbed for...

item.title: LBA: Textual Hard-Label Adversarial Attack under Low Query Budgets
item.description: arXiv:2607.14101v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating high-quality adversarial texts with low query budgets remains a challenging problem in the hard-label scenario. Most existing approaches rely on greedy algorithms, where one position in the text is selected for substitution, followed by the...

item.title: UniSAGE: Unifying Static and Dynamic Attributes with Hyper-Structure
item.description: arXiv:2607.14102v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the rapid growth of digital data, real-world applications increasingly involve hierarchical information that combines static attributes with dynamic records. Modeling such heterogeneous data in a unified and generalizable manner remains...

item.title: Latent Communication Between Language Model Agents: Channels, Alignment, and the Limits of Text
item.description: arXiv:2607.14103v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) are utilized in many contexts and many professions. Those MAS rely on inter-agent communication, usually implemented by clear-text message passing. We hypothesize that Large Language Models may have a world model at their...

item.title: [AINews] Kimi K3 2.8T-A50B: the largest open model ever released; Opus 4.8-class at Sonnet 5 pricing
item.description: a great week for open models continues.

item.title: Elevated errors for Github connector
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational)
